title: NOC 72201 – Industrial electricians | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72201 professionals are the high-level technical specialists who keep Canada’s industrial engine running. As a TEER 2 occupation, Industrial Electricians install, maintain, test, troubleshoot, and repair industrial electrical equipment and associated electrical and electronic controls. In 2026, as Canada’s manufacturing sector transitions to Industry 4.0—utilizing AI-driven robotics and automated assembly lines—Industrial Electricians are classified as "Essential High-Tech Talent." For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Express Entry Category-Based Selection.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group722 – Electrical trades and electrical power line and cable workersMinor Group7220 – Electricians (except industrial and power system)NOC Code72201

duties: Interpretation: Read and interpret drawings, blueprints, schematics, and electrical code specifications to determine layout of industrial electrical equipment installations.

Installation: Install, examine, replace, or repair electrical wiring, receptacles, switch boxes, conduits, feeders, fiber-optic and coaxial cable assemblies, lighting fixtures, and other electrical components.
Equipment Maintenance: Test electrical and electronic equipment and components for continuity, current, voltage, and resistance.
Troubleshooting: Maintain, repair, install, and test switchgear, transformers, switchboard meters, regulators, and reactors.
Automation: Program and troubleshoot Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) and Distributed Control Systems (DCS).
Preventative Maintenance: Operate a preventive maintenance program and keep detailed maintenance records for large-scale industrial machinery.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 4-to-5-year industrial electrician apprenticeship program is the standard route.

Work Experience: Alternatively, a combination of over 5 years of work experience in the trade and some high school, college, or industry courses in industrial electricity may be accepted for trade certification.
Trade Certification (Mandatory/Voluntary): * Trade certification is compulsory in Prince Edward Island, Quebec, and Ontario.It is available, but voluntary, in all other provinces and territories.
It is available, but voluntary, in all other provinces and territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants to ensure national recognition and maximum points for PR through the Federal Skilled Trades Program.
salary_prospects: Note: Licensed Industrial Electricians in the mining or nuclear sectors often earn annual salaries between $105,000 and $135,000 inclusive of overtime and shift premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the transition to "Smart Manufacturing" will drive a 15% growth in specialized industrial electrical roles by 2033. Furthermore, as 20% of the current workforce reaches retirement age by 2030, the demand for journey-level talent to oversee and mentor the next generation of apprentices is a top priority for the Canadian government.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Industrial Electricians in 2026 is Very Good to Critical. In Ontario, Alberta, and Saskatchewan, the expansion of EV battery "Gigafactories," potash mines, and oil-and-gas processing plants has created a severe shortage of specialized journeypersons. According to the 2026 BuildForce and Prism Economics report, industrial employers are currently facing a 72% "Hiring Difficulty" rate for this specific NOC, making it a golden opportunity for international workers with automation experience.
Automation Leadership: Work with the world's most advanced Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs), Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs), and industrial robotic systems.
Economic Security: This trade offers some of the highest stability in the Canadian workforce, with strong presence in essential sectors like mining, energy, and food processing.
Top-Tier Earnings: Industrial Electricians typically earn a significant premium over residential electricians due to the complexity of high-voltage and automated systems.
PR Strategic Advantage: This role is a permanent fixture in Express Entry Trade Category draws, often allowing for invitations with CRS scores 50–100 points lower than the general pool.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary federal route for tradespeople with at least 2 years of work experience.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Skilled Worker Stream): A high-priority route for industrial electricians moving to Ontario’s manufacturing heartland.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for electricians working in the province's resource-rich energy and mining sectors.
